title: A Supreme Court Ruling Threatens America's Wetlands
subtitle: 湿地保护为何关乎你我

paragraphs.en: The miracle of the Chesapeake Bay lies not in its depths, but in the complexity of its natural construction, the interaction of fresh and saline waters, and the mix of land and water. The shallows provide homes for hundreds of species while storing floodwaters, filtering pollutants from water, and protecting nearby communities from potentially destructive storm surges. All this was put at great risk late last month, when the U.S. Supreme Court issued a ruling that provides the EPA far less authority to regulate wetlands and waterways.
paragraphs.zh: 切萨皮克湾的奇迹不在于其深度，而在于其自然构造的复杂性、淡水与咸水的相互作用以及陆地与水域的交融。浅滩为数百种物种提供了家园，同时储存洪水、过滤水中污染物，并保护附近社区免受潜在破坏性风暴潮的侵袭。上月末，这一切都面临巨大风险，因为美国最高法院发布了一项裁决，大大削弱了环保局监管湿地和水道的权力。

paragraphs.en: Specifically, a 5-4 majority decided that wetlands protected by the EPA under its Clean Water Act authority must have a 'continuous surface connection' to bodies of water. This narrowing of the regulatory scope was a victory for builders, mining operators and other commercial interests often at odds with environmental rules. And it carries 'significant repercussions for water quality and flood control throughout the United States,' as Justice Brett Kavanaugh observed.
paragraphs.zh: 具体而言，最高法院以5比4的多数裁定，受环保局根据《清洁水法》授权保护的湿地必须与水体有“连续的地表连接”。监管范围的缩小对建筑商、矿业经营者及其他经常与环境法规相悖的商业利益集团来说是一场胜利。正如布雷特·卡瓦诺法官所指出的，这“对整个美国的水质和防洪产生了重大影响”。

paragraphs.en: In Maryland, the good news is that there are many state laws in place that provide wetlands protections. But that's a very shortsighted view, particularly when it comes to the Chesapeake Bay. The reality is that water, and the pollutants that so often come with it, don't respect state boundaries. The Chesapeake draws from a 64,000-square-mile watershed that extends into Virginia, Pennsylvania, New York, West Virginia, the District of Columbia and Delaware.
paragraphs.zh: 在马里兰州，好消息是有许多州法律在提供湿地保护。但这是一个非常短视的看法，尤其是在涉及切萨皮克湾时。现实是，水以及常常随之而来的污染物，不会尊重州界。切萨皮克湾的水来自一个面积达六万四千平方英里的流域，延伸至弗吉尼亚、宾夕法尼亚、纽约、西弗吉尼亚、哥伦比亚特区和特拉华州。

paragraphs.en: It is too easy, and misleading, to see such court rulings as merely standing up for the rights of land owners when the consequences can be so dire for their neighbors. Pennsylvania farmers, to use one telling example, aren't thinking about next year's blue crab harvest in Maryland when they decide whether to spread animal waste on their fields, yet the runoff into nearby creeks can have enormous impact downstream. We would call on state lawmakers to consider reviewing their own wetlands protections and see for themselves the enormous stakes involved.
paragraphs.zh: 当后果对邻居可能如此严重时，将此类法院裁决仅仅视为维护土地所有者权利，这太容易也具误导性。举一个有说服力的例子，宾夕法尼亚州的农民在决定是否将动物粪便撒在田里时，并不会考虑明年马里兰州蓝蟹的收成，然而流入附近溪流的径流可能对下游产生巨大影响。我们呼吁州立法者考虑审查各自的湿地保护措施，并亲眼看看其中涉及的巨大利害关系。
